description_long: This object is a lush, conical cluster of small, white flowers forming a dense inflorescence, reminiscent of a hydrangea panicle. The flower head is composed of numerous delicate, overlapping petals, creating a soft, cloud-like appearance. At the base, several broad, ovate green leaves emerge, providing a vivid contrast to the white floral mass above. The leaves are smooth, slightly glossy, and have prominent veins, with a central stem supporting both the flower and foliage. The overall structure is upright and compact, with the flower head positioned terminally at the end of the stem. This type of flower is commonly found in urban parks, gardens, and landscaped areas, adding ornamental value to the environment.
description: A dense, conical cluster of small white flowers with broad green leaves and a central supporting stem. The flower head is lush and cloud-like, contrasting with the vivid green foliage below.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full, rounded white flower head and three prominent green leaves at the base.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the conical shape of the flower head and the spread of the leaves, with the stem partially visible.
description_view_2: Angled view displaying the layered petals and the arrangement of leaves, with the flower head slightly tilted.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the flower head's symmetry and the central stem with two main leaves visible.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are densely packed in a whorled arrangement, forming a conical inflorescence with significant overlapping and layering, creating a soft, cloud-like appearance. The structure is radially symmetrical.
